articles.title: Greek Real Estate Partners JV Seals $79M Financing Package for N.J. Industrial Dev
articles.narrative: A joint venture led by Greek Real Estate Partners (GREP) secured a $79 million financing package for the development of a 281,215-square-foot Class A industrial facility in Parsippany, NJ. The financing includes a $49 million recapitalization and a $30 million construction loan provided by Provident Bank. The project, which involves redeveloping a site previously occupied by an office building, is expected to achieve substantial completion in the first quarter of 2027, addressing strong demand for modern logistics space in a constrained market.

data_points.notable_features: Class A industrial facility with 40-foot clear height, 2,500-square-foot speculative office, and 39 loading docks.
data_points.project_notes: The site was previously acquired by GREP in 2023 as an occupied office building. The financing package includes a $49 million recapitalization and a $30 million construction loan.

articles.title: Expanding Floral Distributor Buys Facility in L.A. County
articles.narrative: Holland Flower Market, a flower importer and wholesaler, acquired a 91,010-square-foot Class A industrial facility in Commerce, CA, for $34.1 million to support its expanding operations and investment in automation. The buyer secured a $25 million acquisition loan from BMO Bank. This transaction highlights a trend in Southern California where owner-users are leveraging current market conditions, including softer leasing and a correction in infill rents, to acquire high-quality assets in prime locations.

data_points.notable_features: Class A freestanding industrial facility with six dock-high loading positions, one grade-level door, 32-foot minimum clear heights, a fully secured truck court, and a 9,994-square-foot two-story office component.
data_points.project_notes: The seller, QuadReal Property Group, previously acquired the property for $25.8 million in June 2021.

articles.market_intelligence: The industrial market in Los Angeles has a vacancy rate of 4.2 percent, with leasing activity rebounding in Q1 2026. Infill rents have seen a 26 percent correction from peak levels, accompanied by elevated concessions, creating more favorable conditions for tenants and owner-users.

articles.title: Hawkins Way Capital Drops $28M on 81 East Third Street Apartments, Student Housing
articles.narrative: Hawkins Way Capital has acquired a mixed-use property at 81 East Third Street in New York City for $28 million. The 13-story building includes both student housing and market-rate apartments, with approximately two-thirds of the space currently serving as a dormitory for the New York Conservatory for Dramatic Arts. The student housing component will remain, while the future of the market-rate apartments is yet to be determined. This acquisition follows Hawkins Way Capital's recent purchase of another student housing facility in the Upper West Side.

data_points.address: 81 East Third Street, New York City, NY
data_points.notable_features: Mixed-use property comprising student housing and market-rate apartments; two-thirds occupied by students from the New York Conservatory for Dramatic Arts.
data_points.project_notes: The seller entity redeveloped the building into student and rental housing in 2005.

tenants: New York Conservatory for Dramatic Arts

articles.title: Warsh’s First Fed Meeting as Chair Produces a Result Many in CRE Expected
articles.narrative: The Federal Reserve, under new Chairman Kevin Warsh, unanimously voted to maintain its benchmark interest rate between 3.5 percent and 3.75 percent for the fourth consecutive meeting, signaling a continued 'higher-for-longer' environment due to inflation uncertainty and geopolitical tensions. The updated 'dot plot' projections removed previous forecasts for a rate cut this year and indicated potential rate hikes, with the federal funds rate now projected at 3.8 percent by the end of 2026. While the commercial real estate industry desires lower rates, some market participants believe a quarter-point reduction would not significantly impact deal pricing, and many are prepared to transact. The market has largely priced in these higher-for-longer conditions, with the 10-Year Treasury yield being a more critical factor for refinancing economics than the federal funds rate.

people.name: Jay Neveloff
people.title: Partner and Chair of U.S. Real Estate

articles.title: City Invests $1B for New Affordable Housing Preservation Program
articles.narrative: The New York City Department of Housing Preservation and Development (HPD) has launched the Supportive Preservation Program (SPP), a $1 billion initiative aimed at preserving over 30,000 units of supportive affordable housing across the city. The program will offer tax exemptions, below-market loans, and loan modifications to existing supportive housing projects that have social service contracts with the city. This effort is part of Mayor Zohran Mamdani's broader "Block by Block: The Housing Plan for a New Era," which seeks to create and preserve 400,000 affordable homes over the next decade.

data_points.notable_features: Program targets existing supportive housing projects with city-administered social service contracts.
data_points.project_notes: Part of Mayor Zohran Mamdani's "Block by Block: The Housing Plan for a New Era" aiming for 200,000 new affordable homes and 200,000 preserved homes over 10 years.

companies_people.label: OWNER
companies_people.firm_name: New York City Department of Housing Preservation and Development (HPD)

people.name: Leila Bozorg
people.title: Deputy Mayor for Housing and Planning

articles.title: April Housing Reopens Three Affordable Housing Communities in East Austin Following $60M Rehabilitation
articles.narrative: April Housing, the affordable housing division of Blackstone Real Estate, has completed a $60 million rehabilitation of three affordable housing communities in East Austin, totaling 654 units. The extensive renovations, undertaken in partnership with the Housing Authority of the City of Austin, included updated unit interiors, building systems, and amenity spaces across all properties. These communities provide housing for seniors and families earning between 30% and 80% of the area median income.

data_points.property_name: Heritage Point / Eagle's Landing / Village at Collinswood
data_points.notable_features: Affordable housing for seniors and families earning 30-80% of AMI.
data_points.project_notes: Rehabilitation project across three existing communities.

companies_people.label: OWNER
companies_people.firm_name: Housing Authority of the City of Austin (HACA)

articles.title: Larkspur Capital Begins Leasing Townhome Project in Dallas’ Cedars District
articles.narrative: Larkspur Capital has commenced leasing for Phase I of Victoria Row, a 155-unit townhome development in Dallas' Cedars District. The project, designed by Architecture Demarest and built by Acadian Group, offers one-, two-, and three-bedroom floor plans across 29 buildings on an eight-acre site. Amenities include a pool, dog park, and multiple courtyards, with the developer also recently delivering units in Phase II.

data_points.notable_features: Amenities include a pool, dog park, and multiple courtyards.
data_points.project_notes: Phase I consists of 155 residences across 29 buildings. Phase II units were recently delivered.

articles.title: JLL Arranges Construction Financing for 111,923 SF Industrial Project in North Fort Worth
articles.narrative: JLL has arranged construction financing and an equity investment for Signature 35, a 111,923-square-foot industrial project in North Fort Worth. The facility, a partnership between Indco Partners and Holley Development Co., is expected to be completed in the first quarter of 2027. Simmons Bank provided the construction loan, while an undisclosed family office supplied the equity.

data_points.notable_features: Features 32-foot clear heights, 36 dock doors, 122-foot truck court depths, and two oversized ramped doors.

articles.title: Telecommunications Firm Signs 42,000 SF Lease at Flex Building in Richardson, Texas
articles.narrative: A telecommunications hardware manufacturer has signed a 42,000-square-foot lease for a flex building in Richardson, Texas. The company is relocating from a nearby facility and plans to invest over $2 million in tenant improvements and new equipment for its new space, which will accommodate office, lab, manufacturing, and engineering functions.

data_points.project_notes: Tenant plans to invest over $2 million in tenant improvements and new furniture, fixtures, and equipment.

articles.title: JLL Negotiates Sale of 655,852 SF Industrial Portfolio Near Port of Virginia
articles.narrative: NorthPoint Development has acquired a four-building industrial portfolio totaling 655,852 square feet near the Port of Virginia in the Hampton Roads region. The properties, built between 2005 and 2012, were 82 percent occupied by 10 tenants at the time of sale. JLL represented the seller, an affiliate of Equus Capital Partners Ltd., in the transaction.

data_points.address: 6900, 6920 and 6950 Harbour View Blvd, Suffolk, VA; 2601 Indian River Road, Chesapeake, VA
data_points.notable_features: Portfolio of distribution facilities located near the Port of Virginia.
data_points.project_notes: Properties were built between 2005 and 2012.

articles.title: An insatiable demand? The data center sector still red hot throughout Texas
articles.narrative: Newmark's recent data center outlook report highlights Texas as a prime market for data center development due to its scale, flexibility, and lower-cost power, which supports a robust "powered industries ecosystem." This ecosystem, where digital infrastructure, power generation, advanced manufacturing, and supply chain activities are interconnected, is driving significant industrial demand. The report quantifies that each operating data center in Texas is associated with approximately 531,000 square feet of supporting industrial space, with data-center-related industrial leasing reaching nearly 14 million square feet in 2025. The primary challenges to faster growth include power constraints, grid connection delays, equipment bottlenecks, and increasing community opposition over water use and infrastructure strain. Developers are responding by adopting water-efficient cooling systems and collaborating with local officials to address concerns.

articles.title: The Doran Group begins construction 128-unit seniors housing community in Hudson
articles.narrative: The Doran Group has commenced construction on Linden House – Hudson, a 128-unit seniors housing community in Hudson, Wisconsin. The project, which secured $26 million in construction financing from Veridian Credit Union, represents the launch of the developer's second senior housing brand, targeting regional hubs and growing smaller cities in the Upper Midwest. The community is designed to offer upscale living with a mix of spacious one- and two-bedroom homes, each featuring private outdoor balconies and underground heated parking.

data_points.notable_features: Upscale setting with an outdoor central terrace, pool, clubroom, wellness center, craft spaces, pet run and spa, and grand lobby. Units range from 690 to 1,380 square feet with private outdoor balconies and underground heated parking.
data_points.project_notes: This project marks the launch of a second senior housing brand for The Doran Group, specifically designed for strong regional hubs and growing smaller cities across the Upper Midwest.

articles.title: Celebrating the victories, acknowledging the challenges at Kansas City Industrial Summit
articles.narrative: The Kansas City Industrial Summit, hosted by Midwest Real Estate, brought together industry leaders to discuss the current state of the industrial sector. While acknowledging a softening of demand compared to the post-pandemic boom, speakers emphasized the market's underlying strengths, including its central location, robust labor force, and solid infrastructure. The consensus was that the market has stabilized, with quality industrial space in prime locations continuing to attract interest, indicating a healthy and sustainable environment.

companies_people.label: OTHER
companies_people.firm_name: Kansas City SmartPort + KC Foreign Trade Zones

people.name: Joe Oliaro
people.title: Strategic Real Estate & Business Development Executive

people.name: John Hassler
people.title: Executive Managing Director, Industrial & Logistics – Heartland Region

people.name: Andrew Perkins
people.title: Director of Commercial Real Estate Lending, SVP

articles.title: Clear Height Properties closes disposition of 354,000-square-foot manufacturing facility in Kenosha
articles.narrative: Clear Height Properties and Buligo Capital Partners have sold a 354,000-square-foot manufacturing and distribution facility located in Kenosha, Wisconsin. The disposition follows Clear Height's successful execution of a long-term lease extension with a corporate guarantee, which stabilized the property and enhanced its investment-grade credit profile. The facility's strategic location offers strong connectivity to both the Chicago and Milwaukee metropolitan areas.

data_points.notable_features: Equipped with integrated labs, lighting paint lines, and customized production infrastructure.
data_points.project_notes: Clear Height executed a long-term lease extension with a corporate guarantee and S&P A- investment grade credit enhancement prior to sale.

articles.title: Lawrence Group completes work on free-standing clinic in Austin
articles.narrative: Lawrence Group has completed the development of ARC South Specialty, a new 63,000-square-foot, two-level free-standing clinic in Austin, TX, for Austin Regional Clinic (ARC). The facility, which opened on June 1, consolidates a wide range of specialty, primary, and surgical care services into a single campus location. Designed with a patient-centered approach, the clinic incorporates locally sourced materials and sustainable features, aiming for an Austin Energy Green Building 3 Stars rating.

data_points.address: 708 West Ben White Blvd., Austin, TX
data_points.notable_features: Designed to achieve an Austin Energy Green Building (AEGB) 3 Stars rating; incorporates locally sourced limestone veneer and wood panels; includes lab and radiology services, plus cardiology labs.
data_points.project_notes: Lawrence Group performed a detailed feasibility study in June 2024, which led ARC to opt for new construction over adaptive reuse. The facility will also house ARC Surgery Center South, expected to open in summer 2026.

articles.title: Rockpoint, LCOR plot historic 299-unit resi complex in Georgetown
articles.narrative: Rockpoint, LCOR, and Potomac Investment Properties are partnering in a joint venture to redevelop two existing properties at 1000 and 1050 Thomas Jefferson Street NW in Washington, D.C.'s Georgetown neighborhood. This project will create 299 rental units across two buildings, marking the largest multifamily development in Georgetown in over a century. The development will also include 18,000 square feet of retail space and preserve existing parking.

data_points.address: 1000 and 1050 Thomas Jefferson Street NW, Washington, D.C.
data_points.notable_features: Proposed amenities include resident lounges, a rooftop pool, a fitness center, a pet spa, and a library. The project will also include 18,000 square feet of retail space and preserve 282 below-grade parking spaces.
data_points.project_notes: The joint venture received approval for the conversion from the Old Georgetown Board and the U.S. Commission of Fine Arts. This is the first multifamily project in Georgetown to exceed 150 units in over a century.

articles.market_intelligence: Washington, D.C. has become a hub for conversions, with 32 adaptive reuse projects currently underway, totaling approximately 7 million square feet. This is a significant increase from the average of four conversions per year between 2016 and 2024.

articles.title: Fed’s real estate portfolio needs more than $25B of maintenance
articles.narrative: The federal government's real estate portfolio, managed by the General Services Administration (GSA), faces a significant deferred maintenance challenge, estimated at $25.8 billion. This includes 62 buildings requiring over $100 million in repairs, with two Washington D.C. buildings needing more than $1 billion each. The poor condition of these properties hinders the government's efforts to sell them, while legislative hurdles, such as the $4 million prospectus threshold requiring congressional approval for repairs, cause delays and increased costs. Additionally, Congress has diverted over $15 billion from the Federal Buildings Fund, further exacerbating the maintenance funding shortfall.

data_points.notable_features: Includes the Agriculture Building and Herbert Hoover Building, both requiring over $1 billion in deferred maintenance.
data_points.project_notes: The Public Buildings Reform Board estimated deferred maintenance at $50 billion in March. Over $1 billion in proposed repairs were not completed in fiscal year 2025 due to delayed congressional approval.

articles.title: CoStar hit with another antitrust lawsuit, alleging price-fixing of commercial rents
articles.narrative: CoStar Group and several major commercial brokerages, including CBRE, Colliers, Cushman & Wakefield, JLL, and Newmark, are facing a proposed class-action antitrust lawsuit. A commercial tenant alleges a "hub-and-spoke" conspiracy where CoStar gathered and disseminated sensitive lease data, enabling brokerages to align asking rents and reduce concessions, resulting in artificially inflated rents. This lawsuit follows similar antitrust litigation against RealPage concerning multifamily rents and raises broader questions about the legality of sharing aggregated rent data.

articles.title: Pulte, Tri Pointe pay $63M for state-owned land near Phoenix as prices fall
articles.narrative: PulteGroup and Tri Pointe Homes have finalized the acquisition of a 274-acre parcel from the state of Arizona for $63 million, completing their land assembly for the 1,200-acre Aloravita master-planned residential community near Phoenix. The joint venture plans to develop 568 homes on this site, with Tri Pointe building 275 and Pulte constructing 293, to be delivered in phases. This acquisition follows their successful bid in October 2024 and subsequent entitlement work with the City of Peoria.

data_points.project_notes: The 274-acre parcel is part of the larger 1,200-acre Aloravita residential community. PulteGroup first started assembling its Aloravita package in 2017. Model home construction is expected to begin late 2027.

articles.market_intelligence: The Phoenix market is balanced, with the median list price down 7.3 percent year-over-year in April to $475,000. Homes are sitting on the market an average of 54 days.

articles.title: Top Aspen agent joins Douglas Elliman
articles.narrative: Maureen Stapleton, a prominent real estate agent in Aspen, has joined Douglas Elliman, bringing with her a significant listing and plans to build a team. Her move follows a record-setting year where she brokered one of Aspen's most expensive residential sales, a Red Mountain estate that traded for over $90 million. Stapleton's transition is part of Douglas Elliman's broader strategy to expand its presence in the mountain region, including key markets like Denver and Sun Valley, Idaho, capitalizing on the active yet supply-constrained high-end market in Aspen.

people.name: Scott Durkin
people.title: Head of the firm's brokerage division

articles.market_intelligence: Aspen's high-end market is described as "very active" but suffers from "extremely low" inventory, which has contributed to rising prices. Douglas Elliman is actively expanding its presence across the "mountain region," including Denver, Telluride, Sun Valley, Idaho, and Utah.

articles.title: NYC’s top deals: Brooklyn school trades for $16M
articles.narrative: Eastern Union Properties sold a three-story school building in Crown Heights, Brooklyn, for $16.1 million to an affiliate of the Educational Institute Oholei Torah of Brooklyn. The property spans over 57,800 square feet, translating to approximately $280 per square foot. Additionally, Strong Equities sold a 15,600-square-foot retail property in the East Tremont section of the Bronx for $10 million to 761 East Tremont Avenue LLC.

data_points.address: 402 Eastern Parkway, New York City, NY
data_points.notable_features: Three-story building.

companies_people.label: BUYER
companies_people.firm_name: Educational Institute Oholei Torah of Brooklyn

articles.title: “Podfather” drops $17M at Extell’s Lincoln Square tower
articles.narrative: Jon Rubinstein, a key engineer behind the original iPod, has acquired a luxury condominium at Extell Development's 50 West 66th Street in Lincoln Square for $17.1 million. The unit, spanning approximately 2,800 square feet, features three bedrooms, three bathrooms, and a private loggia with views of the Hudson River and Central Park. This sale is a positive indicator for the luxury market amidst concerns about a potential pied-à-terre tax, as the building is now over 80 percent sold.

data_points.address: 50 West 66th Street, New York City, NY
data_points.notable_features: Features a 140-square-foot private loggia with views of the Hudson River and Central Park. Building amenities include an indoor saltwater lap pool, sauna, steam room, full-size basketball and pickleball courts, and an outdoor saltwater pool and hot tub on the 20th floor.

articles.market_intelligence: In May, Manhattan saw 90 contracts signed for homes asking at least $5 million, which was 10 percent above the May 10-year average. Another unit at 50 West 66th Street, Unit 41E, sold for approximately $47 million in December.

articles.title: Former architect sentenced for Gilgo Beach killing spree
articles.narrative: Rex Heuermann, a former architect, has been sentenced to multiple life sentences for a series of murders on Long Island. He pleaded guilty to three counts of first-degree murder and four counts of second-degree murder, also admitting to an eighth murder. Heuermann, who owned RH Consultants & Associates for 30 years, will be transferred to a state prison and has agreed to cooperate with the FBI's Behavioral Analysis Unit.

data_points.address: 385 Fifth Avenue, New York City, NY

articles.title: Council members: No CityFHEPS expansion? No budget
articles.narrative: New York City Council members are threatening to reject any budget deal that does not include an expansion of the CityFHEPS housing voucher program. Despite campaign promises, Mayor Zohran Mamdani's budget proposal actually cuts $500 million from the program, primarily by reducing payouts to brokers. Council members argue that expanding CityFHEPS, which helps prevent homelessness by covering a portion of eligible tenants' rent, would ultimately save the city money by reducing shelter costs. The program's costs have tripled in the last three years, projected to reach $1.7 billion this fiscal year, with a full expansion estimated to cost an additional $6 billion to $20 billion over five years.

articles.title: Ex-Christie’s tri-state affiliate accused of withholding commissions
articles.market: New Jersey / New York City, NY
articles.narrative: A former Christie's International Real Estate affiliate, now operating as The Agency One Rock, is facing allegations of widespread delayed commission payments to its agents. The issues reportedly led to Christie's International Real Estate terminating its licensing agreement with the firm. Several current and former agents claim they waited months for payments, with one agent estimating he is owed $60,000. The firm is also defending against a lawsuit from a former agent alleging unpaid commissions and bogus charges, though the firm has filed counterclaims.

articles.title: South Florida’s top deals: Edgewater development site trades for $43M
articles.narrative: Cain and Kushner acquired a 1.5-acre apartment development site in Miami's Edgewater neighborhood for $43.1 million from a joint venture between OKO Group and Cain. The buyers plan to develop a 40-story, 364-unit luxury residential project on the site. This transaction represents a significant investment in the area's growing multifamily market.

articles.title: Kolter starts Delray Live Local rental project with $92M financing
articles.narrative: Kolter Multifamily has commenced construction on Alton Delray, a 386-unit multifamily development in Delray Beach, after securing $91.7 million in financing from U.S. Bank. The project, located at 2101 South Congress Avenue, will dedicate 40% of its units to workforce housing under Florida's Live Local Act, allowing the developer to proceed without rezoning on the industrial-zoned site. The development will feature five mid-rise buildings totaling 460,000 square feet, with amenities including a clubhouse, pool, and dog park, and is expected to deliver its first apartments in late 2027.

data_points.address: 2101 South Congress Avenue, Delray Beach, FL
data_points.notable_features: 40% of units (155 units) dedicated to workforce housing under Florida's Live Local Act; amenities include a clubhouse, pool, walking trail, and dog park.
data_points.project_notes: Site previously acquired for $21.9 million in 2024 from the George T. Elmore Trust.

articles.title: Bass Pro Shops rings up Cheeca Lodge resort in Florida Keys
articles.narrative: Bass Pro Shops has acquired the iconic Cheeca Lodge & Spa, a luxury fishing and sporting resort in Islamorada, Florida, from Northwood Investors. The 27-acre property, known for its rich history and amenities, was previously marketed for $300 million. Northwood Hospitality will continue to manage the resort, which has undergone significant upgrades since its acquisition by Northwood Investors in 2011, including the addition of oceanfront suites, luxury casitas, and a conference center.

data_points.notable_features: Luxury fishing and sporting resort with three restaurants, a store, a spa, a kids camp, six tennis courts, and a 525-foot fishing pier. It is one of the most famous fishing resorts in the country.
data_points.project_notes: Northwood Investors previously acquired the property in 2011 and expanded it in 2020 with the acquisition of neighboring Casitas and Morada Bay properties.

articles.title: Steve Ross buying Boca Raton’s ex-IBM campus, plots mixed-use “micro city”
articles.narrative: Related Ross is nearing a deal to acquire the 1.7 million-square-foot Boca Raton Innovation Campus (BRiC) in Boca Raton, FL, with plans to transform the former IBM campus into a mixed-use "micro city." The acquisition leverages existing 2023 city approvals for a multi-phased development that could include over 1,200 residential units, medical offices, retail space, an entertainment venue, and a potential 140-key hotel. The campus, known for its brutalist architecture and historical significance as the birthplace of the first personal computer, has attracted new tenants due to South Florida's business-friendly environment.

data_points.notable_features: Former IBM campus where the first personal computer was reportedly invented, known for its brutalist architecture.
data_points.project_notes: Existing entitlements from 2023 allow for a multi-phased development including residential, medical offices, retail, an entertainment venue, and a hotel. The site was previously acquired in 2018 for $179.3 million and a stake was sold in 2021 for $320.2 million.

articles.title: Inside litigation surrounding Amrit Ocean Resort condo-hotel
articles.narrative: This article details the ongoing litigation surrounding the Amrit Ocean Resort, a condo-hotel on Singer Island, Florida. Unit owners have filed multiple lawsuits against the developer, alleging issues ranging from delayed turnover of the condo association board to construction defects and disputes over homestead exemptions. The developer maintains that the project's governing documents and operational structure were properly established and that they are working to resolve the issues.

data_points.address: 3100 North Ocean Drive, Riviera Beach, FL
data_points.notable_features: Oceanfront condo-hotel with a four-story spa and wellness focus.
data_points.project_notes: Project relaunched in 2017 after a previous version was canceled a decade prior due to a condo market downturn. Buyers began closing on units in October 2023.

companies_people.label: OWNER
companies_people.firm_name: Amrit Ocean Resort II Condominium Association

articles.title: West Dundee pitches 100-acre Spring Hill Mall site for redevelopment
articles.narrative: West Dundee is actively seeking developers for the 100-acre former Spring Hill Mall site, presenting two conceptual mixed-use models to attract interest. The village invested $13 million to acquire various mall parcels, including those previously owned by Kohan Retail Investment Group, and subsequently demolished the 1980s-era shopping center to prepare the land for redevelopment. The proposed concepts prioritize housing density and retail, with one model suggesting 778 housing units and 320,000 square feet of retail, and another featuring 1,326 residential units and 400,000 square feet of retail, both incorporating pedestrian-focused streetscapes and park space. Village officials anticipate a redevelopment timeline of up to 10 years, emphasizing that the models are starting points for discussion with potential developers.

data_points.address: 2000 Spring Hill Mall, West Dundee, IL
data_points.notable_features: Conceptual models propose either 778 housing units and 320,000 SF of retail, or 1,326 residential units and 400,000 SF of retail, with pedestrian-focused streetscapes and a park.
data_points.project_notes: West Dundee spent $13 million to acquire multiple mall parcels. The 1980s-era shopping center was demolished last year to prepare the site for construction. The full redevelopment timeline is expected to span 10 years.

articles.market_intelligence: Municipalities across Chicagoland are taking direct control of distressed retail hubs for redevelopment, such as Bloomingdale acquiring Stratford Square Mall for $17 million. Private players are also active, with Xroads Real Estate Advisors purchasing Lincolnwood Town Center for $12.3 million for mixed-use redevelopment. Other projects include the proposed $440 million overhaul of Golf Mill Town Center in Niles and residential additions at Westfield Old Orchard in Skokie and Yorktown Center in Lombard. Ford City Mall in Chicago is slated for a retail-to-industrial conversion, with Kurv Industrial under contract to acquire the 66-acre property for $150 million to build a 913,000 SF logistics campus.

articles.title: Brandon Johnson floats $425M taxpayer assist for Related Midwest’s The 78
articles.narrative: Chicago Mayor Brandon Johnson has proposed a $425 million tax increment financing (TIF) package to fund infrastructure improvements at The 78, a 62-acre megadevelopment site in the South Loop. This public subsidy is intended to facilitate the construction of a $750 million, 22,000-seat soccer stadium for the Chicago Fire, which is planned as a privately financed project. The TIF funds would also prepare the broader site for future mixed-use development, including a significant portion allocated for a 1,200-space underground parking garage deemed crucial for the site's challenging topography and as a foundation for future high-rise construction.

data_points.notable_features: Long-stalled 62-acre megadevelopment site along the South Branch of the Chicago River.
data_points.project_notes: The TIF package includes $199 million for public structures, plazas and open space, $104 million for new roads and streetscape work, $34 million for Metra-related improvements, and $30 million for river wall work. The city would own and operate the proposed 1,200-space underground parking garage.

articles.title: Double-dipping: Walker & Dunlop alleges investor committed “fraudulent misrepresentation” to get inflated loans for West Side Chicago apartments
articles.narrative: Walker & Dunlop, on behalf of Freddie Mac, has filed two foreclosure lawsuits against multifamily investor Chaim Bialostozky, alleging "fraudulent misrepresentation" in securing inflated loans for West Side Chicago apartment properties. The lawsuits claim Bialostozky orchestrated same-day, back-to-back sales at higher prices to obtain larger Freddie Mac loans, without disclosing the initial, lower-priced transactions to the lender. The properties, which include a four-building portfolio and a separate apartment building, have allegedly fallen into severe disrepair, with Bialostozky ceasing debt payments last July. Walker & Dunlop is seeking at least $7.1 million to cover unpaid debt and legal fees, and is requesting a receiver be appointed to manage the properties, which require an estimated $7.8 million in repairs.

data_points.project_notes: The lawsuits allege that Chaim Bialostozky purchased a four-building portfolio for $4.6 million, which had traded for $3.5 million earlier the same day, and secured a $3.4 million loan. A separate property at 45-52 North Waller Avenue was purchased for $3.6 million, after an earlier $2.7 million transaction, with a $2.9 million loan secured. The properties are in severe disrepair, requiring an estimated $7.8 million in repairs.

articles.market_intelligence: Multifamily distress has been increasing in Chicago's South and West Sides, where out-of-state investors acquired apartment buildings when interest rates were low and subsequently defaulted on debt payments. Walker & Dunlop previously disclosed potential exposure to up to $100 million in fraudulent mortgages.

articles.title: Murphy, Creek Lane list Fulton Market office amid debt distress
articles.narrative: A joint venture between Murphy Development Group and Creek Lane Capital is marketing its 104,900-square-foot office building in Chicago's Fulton Market, following the maturity of a $43.3 million loan from PGIM Real Estate. The developers completed the property in 2020, and it is currently 78% leased with a weighted average lease term of 7.6 years. The sale is expected to test investor appetite for discounted assets in the Fulton Market area, as the property is likely to trade below its original mortgage value.

data_points.notable_features: Eight-story office building completed in 2020.
data_points.project_notes: Loan from PGIM Real Estate matured in May 2025.

articles.market_intelligence: Spear Street Capital recently acquired an office building at 1201 West Lake Street for approximately $275 per square foot. The 1K Fulton office building across the street is losing at least one tenant.

articles.title: Morgan Stanley eyes Uptown Dallas site for office tower
articles.narrative: Morgan Stanley is reportedly considering a site at 2401 McKinney Avenue in Uptown Dallas for a new office tower. The proposed development would involve demolishing existing structures to make way for a large office building, potentially accommodating thousands of employees. This move is seen as a significant boost for the Uptown submarket, which is facing the departure of several major businesses to suburban locations.

data_points.notable_features: Proposed office tower could accommodate 2,000 to 3,300 financial services employees.
data_points.project_notes: Existing seafood restaurant and defunct gym on the site will be demolished for the new development.

articles.market_intelligence: Dallas' urban core is experiencing a trend of businesses relocating to suburbs like Plano, with the Dallas Mavericks, Dallas Stars, Neiman Marcus, and AT&T all planning departures. AT&T's move alone will leave 2 million square feet of office space vacant in Downtown Dallas, representing about 6 percent of the submarket's total.

articles.title: Compass grows Austin presence with North Mopac office lease
articles.narrative: Compass, a national residential real estate brokerage, has expanded its presence in Austin, TX, by leasing 12,000 square feet at Stonebridge, a two-building office campus. This new lease at 9600 North Mopac Expressway consolidates two smaller offices, increasing the firm's total footprint in the market. The expansion reflects Compass's consistent agent growth and broader regional expansion efforts across Central Texas.

data_points.notable_features: Located on the northeast side of Austin, part of a two-building office campus totaling 690,500 square feet.
data_points.project_notes: This lease consolidates two smaller offices for Compass, increasing their cumulative office space in Austin.

companies_people.label: LANDLORD
companies_people.firm_name: Stonebridge Plaza I & II Texas LLC

articles.title: UBS offloads mostly vacant Downtown Houston office building ripe for conversion
articles.narrative: UBS Realty Investors has sold a largely vacant 24-story office building in Downtown Houston to Tampa-based apartment developer Zhukovskyi Development. The 542,000-square-foot property at 919 Milam Street, built in 1956 and last renovated in 2023, is less than 20 percent occupied and is considered a prime candidate for residential conversion. While sale terms were not disclosed, the property was last valued at $48.3 million for tax purposes. This transaction highlights the ongoing trend of office-to-residential conversions aimed at addressing high office vacancy rates in cities like Houston.

data_points.notable_features: Building has large floor plates of 22,500 square feet, nearly double the size recommended for conversion, but was identified as ripe for apartment conversion in a November 2023 report by Aecom.
data_points.project_notes: Property was last renovated in 2023. The building was previously acquired in 2025 by 3L Real Estate from special servicers Midland, with plans for a $26.5 million apartment conversion.

articles.market_intelligence: Houston's office vacancy rate remains elevated at about 27 percent. Residential conversion is lauded as a solution for older office space, but financial and logistical challenges have limited successful project executions. Texas Senate Bill 840, passed in 2025, aims to ease conversions by removing zoning change requirements.

articles.title: Pearson Ranch flattens with Tri Pointe development agreement
articles.narrative: Tri Pointe Homes has secured approval from Williamson County to develop 95 acres of the 156-acre Pearson Ranch, a significant shift from the project's initial vision as a dense mixed-use community. The homebuilder's plans for residential neighborhoods will largely replace the previously proposed commercial components, including office space, hotels, and retail. While the original developer, Inspire Development, still owns the land, Tri Pointe is set to take control of nearly two-thirds of the site for its residential build-out. This development marks a pivot towards more horizontal residential growth in a project initially envisioned with a $2 billion mixed-use scope.

data_points.notable_features: Located approximately 20 miles north of Downtown Austin, with nearby employers including Dell and Apple.
data_points.project_notes: The 156-acre site was initially proposed in January 2022 as a $2 billion mixed-use development by Inspire Development, including a 48-acre office campus, two hotels, 200,000 SF of retail, and 2,500 multifamily units. The Eden, a 306-unit multifamily community, began construction in 2022 as part of the original plan.

articles.title: Forget the Alamo, remember the “Dellionaires”: What SpaceX’s IPO means for Texas real estate
articles.narrative: This article analyzes the potential impact of SpaceX's recent IPO on the luxury residential real estate markets in Austin and the Rio Grande Valley, Texas. Drawing parallels to the "Dellionaire" boom in Austin following Dell's 1988 IPO, real estate agents anticipate a significant increase in demand for high-end housing from newly minted millionaires. While Austin's established luxury market is expected to see activity in the $3 million to $10 million range, the Rio Grande Valley, particularly Brownsville, is bracing for an unprecedented surge in demand for properties over $1 million, likely necessitating new construction due to limited existing inventory.

articles.title: Silicon Valley rents to rise as much as 7% over the next year: CoStar
articles.narrative: Silicon Valley's multifamily market is poised for significant rent growth, with projections indicating a 6-7% increase over the next year, driven by the booming artificial intelligence sector. This surge in demand, coupled with a dramatic slowdown in new apartment construction, is expected to push vacancy rates to a decade-low of approximately 3% across Santa Clara and San Benito counties. Despite incentives from cities like San Jose and San Francisco to stimulate development, high interest rates, tight capital markets, and elevated construction costs continue to hinder new supply, creating a landlord-favorable environment.

articles.title: Dishelved San Jose site proposed for offices, then housing, falls into default
articles.narrative: A blighted commercial site in San Jose, previously slated for office and then residential development, has fallen into default on a $6.7 million loan. The property, which was acquired in 2018, had a 75-unit apartment project with ground-floor retail proposed in 2024, and was subsequently listed for sale. The site's deteriorating condition has drawn the attention of city code enforcement.

data_points.address: 335 South Winchester Boulevard, San Jose, CA
data_points.project_notes: The 2.6-acre site was previously acquired for $1.5 million in 2018. An initial proposal for an office building was changed in 2024 to a 75-unit apartment project with ground-floor retail. The property was listed for sale with an initial asking price of $25 million, later reduced to $20 million.

articles.market_intelligence: Commercial property defaults in San Jose have been increasing, particularly in the hotel sector. The 52-room Fontaine Inn in east San Jose defaulted on a $6.5 million loan, and a 204-room Motel 6 and Super 8 by Wyndham complex nearby is in default on a $21.7 million loan. The La Quinta Inn & Suites in San Jose also defaulted on a $16 million loan last month.

articles.title: SF’s great AI lease-up continues with latest firm growing nearly sixfold in SoMa
articles.narrative: LangChain, an AI tools company, has significantly expanded its San Francisco office footprint by subleasing 70,000 square feet at 303 Second Street in the South of Market neighborhood from Sony Interactive Entertainment. This move represents a nearly sixfold increase in space for LangChain, which previously occupied 11,800 square feet nearby. The transaction highlights the continued strong demand from artificial intelligence companies for office space in San Francisco, a trend that is driving the city's office market recovery.

data_points.notable_features: The building is a 785,000-square-foot office structure.
data_points.project_notes: LangChain's new lease comes about a year after it moved into an 11,800-square-foot office at 501 Second Street. Sony PlayStation signed a lease for 130,000 square feet across three floors in the building in 2019, and LangChain's sublease includes two of those three floors.

articles.market_intelligence: Tenant requirements for office space in San Francisco are up to approximately 9.7 million square feet, a 50 percent increase from a year ago. Tech firms account for roughly half of this demand, with AI companies alone making up about a quarter. Overall demand has returned to 2018 levels.

articles.title: Kalthia buys another distressed Bay Area hotel months after foreclosed loan sale
articles.narrative: Kalthia Group Hotels, through an affiliate, has acquired the 142-room Hyatt House Pleasant Hill in Pleasant Hill, CA, for $3.3 million. This transaction follows the hotel's foreclosure by a Mount Street U.S. affiliate in June 2025, which subsequently auctioned off the loan in March for $3.5 million. The sale price of approximately $23,200 per room is significantly below the Bay Area's median hotel price per room, reflecting the continued distress in the region's hospitality market.

data_points.address: 2611 Contra Costa Boulevard, Pleasant Hill, CA
data_points.notable_features: Distressed asset acquired months after foreclosure.
data_points.project_notes: The lender, an affiliate of Mount Street U.S., took control of the property through foreclosure in June 2025 and auctioned the loan in March for $3.5 million.

articles.market_intelligence: Northern California hotels traded at a median price of about $109,000 per room in 2025. San Jose's Hotel De Anza traded for $11.5 million, or about $115,000 per room, in 2024. Several Bay Area properties have entered foreclosure over the past year, including a 276-room dual-branded Marriott hotel at 1431 Jefferson Street in Oakland, the 541-room Signia by Hilton in downtown San Jose, and the 500-room Oakland Marriott City Center.

articles.title: Law Firm Fenwick & West Signs Lease Extension, Expansion
articles.narrative: Law firm Fenwick & West, LLP has expanded its office footprint at 902 Broadway in New York City, signing an 11-year lease extension and expansion. The firm added 18,200 square feet, bringing its total occupancy to 72,800 square feet across four floors. The expansion reflects the firm's continued growth and need for additional space in the city, with ownership noting strong interest from professional organizations in premier locations with efficient floorplates.

data_points.project_notes: Fenwick & West originally occupied 54,600 SF across three floors, now totaling 72,800 SF.

articles.title: Farallon Partnership Trades Historic Hawthorne Hangar Campus
articles.narrative: A partnership led by Farallon Capital Management, Jofa Capital, and Urban Investment Research Corporation (URC) has sold the historic Hawthorne Hangar Campus in Hawthorne, CA, for $32.5 million. The industrial flex/R&D property, which served as the original office and airport hangar for aviation pioneer Jack Northrop, was acquired by the selling partnership in 2023 for $13.35 million out of bankruptcy. The sellers secured a long-term lease with a Fortune 100 tenant prior to the sale, aligning with their investment strategy for unique, fragmented assets.

data_points.notable_features: Historic property that served as the original office and airport hangar of Jack Northrop, founder of Northrop Corporation.
data_points.project_notes: The selling partnership previously acquired the asset out of bankruptcy for $13.35 million in 2023.

companies_people.label: SELLER BROKER
companies_people.firm_name: CBRE’s Airport and Aviation Properties Practice Group

articles.title: Global Holdings Wraps Work on 99 Park Avenue Repositioning
articles.narrative: Global Holdings has completed a $30 million repositioning of 99 Park Avenue, a 600,000-square-foot Class A office tower in New York City. The project, designed in collaboration with Vocon, focused on enhancing the building's arrival experience while preserving its Art Deco character. The building is currently 97% leased, reflecting strong tenant interest in the upgraded space.

data_points.address: 99 Park Avenue, New York City, NY
data_points.notable_features: Class A office tower near Grand Central Terminal, Art Deco heritage, reimagined arrival experience.
data_points.project_notes: Strategic repositioning project.

people.name: Chris Roth
people.title: COO and Head of Office Asset Management

articles.title: MBK Opens Murrieta Rentals Following Strong Demand in Pre-Leasing Phase
articles.narrative: MBK Rental Living has officially opened Vintage Farms, a 330-unit apartment community in Murrieta, CA, following strong pre-leasing demand. The developer emphasized creating a differentiated community with modern amenities and a strong connection to its natural surroundings, integrating hillside views and outdoor gathering spaces into the resident experience. The project also incorporates sustainable features, reflecting the company's commitment to environmentally conscious development.

data_points.notable_features: Community features EV charging stations, energy-efficient LED lighting and Energy Star appliances, low or no VOC materials, bicycle racks, and a weekly recycling program.

articles.title: Union Investment, Nuveen Sell Upper East Side Retail
articles.narrative: Union Investment and Nuveen Real Estate have sold their 1511 Third Ave. retail property on Manhattan’s Upper East Side to Stockbridge. The fully leased property spans approximately 17,500 square feet across four floors and a basement. The sale was executed for strategic portfolio reasons and to enhance the fund's liquidity, amidst a transaction market characterized by limited activity and high interest rates.

data_points.address: 1511 Third Ave., New York City, NY
data_points.notable_features: Fully leased property spanning four floors plus a basement.
data_points.project_notes: Union Investment acquired the retail property in 2016 as part of a closed joint venture deal with Nuveen Real Estate for its open-ended real estate fund Unilmmo: Global, which held a 49% stake.

people.name: Kseniya Merritt
people.title: SVP and head of retail investments North America

articles.title: Warsh Wants A 'New Chapter' For The Fed. A Rate Hike Could Come First
articles.narrative: Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh, in his first press conference, outlined a comprehensive plan to reform the central bank's operations through five new task forces, signaling a "new chapter" for the Fed. This initiative comes as inflation significantly exceeds the Fed's target and the labor market shows strong but shallow job gains, leading to expectations of a potential rate hike this year. While the benchmark rate was held flat, Warsh's emphasis on reassessment and a more proactive policy approach, rather than a data-dependent and reactive one, suggests a significant shift in the Fed's strategy for navigating the current economic landscape.

people.name: Jeff Palma
people.title: Head of Multi-Asset and Macro Research

articles.title: 'An Avalanche Of Good': Scottish World Cup Crowds Have Boosted Boston Businesses
articles.narrative: The influx of an estimated 50,000 Scottish World Cup fans has provided a significant economic boost to Boston's downtown businesses, including hotels, bars, restaurants, and retail. Despite initial concerns about international tourism and lower-than-forecast hotel bookings in host cities, Boston experienced strong demand, with some establishments tripling sales and hotels seeing increased average rates. This surge in activity, compared to the Celtics' championship parade, highlights the importance of destination-worthy events in revitalizing the city's downtown, which has faced challenges with affordability and nightlife perceptions. Business leaders hope the World Cup's momentum will continue to attract tourists and further stimulate the local economy.
articles.market_intelligence: Boston hotel occupancy on Friday and Saturday averaged 79% and 82% respectively, below the occupancy on the same days last year, but average rates were up 28% and 29% those days, and revenue per available room (RevPAR) was up 23% and 24%. Boston ranked No. 2 among the 11 U.S. World Cup host cities for growth in the number of people hosting on Airbnb, with Scottish travelers accounting for nearly a quarter of bookings around their game against Haiti.

articles.title: Hackman Capital Faces $258M Default
articles.narrative: Hackman Capital Partners has defaulted on a $257.6 million loan from Deutsche Bank for its MBS Media Campus in Manhattan Beach, CA, potentially leading to foreclosure. This default is part of a broader trend affecting Hackman Capital's entertainment-focused properties, which have been impacted by a significant slowdown in filming and television production in Southern California. The company has faced multiple defaults and foreclosures on other studio assets in recent months, reflecting the challenging market conditions for studio owners.

data_points.project_notes: The property was previously acquired by Hackman Capital Partners from the Carlyle Group in 2019 for $650 million. Deutsche Bank originated a $247.5 million loan on the property in August 2019.

articles.title: Multifamily Starts Tumble 40% As Housing Construction Hits 6-Year Low
articles.narrative: New multifamily construction starts experienced a significant decline last month, falling 40.2% from April and reaching an annualized pace of 295,000 units, a 14.2% decrease from May 2025. This contributed to overall housing starts hitting a six-year low at less than 1.2 million units annually, far below economists' projections. The downturn is attributed to persistent financing challenges, elevated interest rates, and rising construction and property insurance costs, leading builders to remain cautious despite some resilience in the Midwest.

people.name: Jing Fu
people.title: Senior Director of Forecasting and Analysis
